answers.answer: I predicted more bubbles near the lamp because light supplies energy for the light-dependent reactions, and equal or higher counts far away would have challenged that prediction.
Equal bicarbonate reduced carbon availability as a competing explanation, so lamp distance was the intended difference rather than unequal dissolved carbon.
The means fell from eighteen to twelve to seven as distance increased, while temperature stayed stable, so the ordered pattern supports the hypothesis within this setup.
The result proves light is the sole factor everywhere, but I cannot explain how these three cuttings ruled out carbon, temperature, or plant differences in other conditions.
